_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: wilson(SC-AG/running-Gov) skrmetti(TN-AG/appointed) matos(RI-LG) venhuizen(SD-LG) jackley(SD-AG/running-US-House). Cited; candidacy corrections for Wilson/Skrmetti/Jackley.

profile.religion: Presbyterian (Saxe Gotha Presbyterian Church, Lexington SC)
profile.confidence_note: Scored from SC AG official actions/press releases + iVoterGuide survey + campaign site (2026-06). CANDIDACY CORRECTION: not seeking AG reelection — running for SC Governor; advanced to 2026-06-23 GOP runoff vs. Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette.

alan-wilson.notes_set: SC AG (51st, since 2011), 4 terms. Running for GOVERNOR 2026 (open seat, McMaster term-limited); June 9 GOP primary no majority → June 23 runoff vs. Evette. DEVIATION on abortion: pledged to VETO S.1095 (no-exceptions ban), supports keeping rape/incest exceptions — restrictionist, not abolitionist. HARD AG record on religious liberty, 2A/ATF litigation, anti-illegal-immigration suits, anti-gender-ideology/parental rights, voter-ID, ESG pushback. De-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: Self-describes 'unequivocally pro-life'; defended SC 6-week Heartbeat Act.

1.note: Pledged to VETO S.1095 no-exceptions ban; keeps rape/incest exceptions — restrictionist, not abolitionist.

2.note: No public position on embryonic/IVF/chimeric research.

3.note: No public position on euthanasia/assisted suicide.

4.note: Led repeated litigation against Planned Parenthood; no abortion-industry PAC funding found.

profile.religion: Church of Christ (Protestant; attends Harpeth Hills Church of Christ, TN)
profile.confidence_note: Scored from TN AG official actions/filings + public remarks (2022-2026). CANDIDACY CORRECTION: TN AG is APPOINTED by the state Supreme Court (Aug 2022) to an 8-year term ending 2030-09-01 — not popularly elected; prior 'primary 2026-08-04' field was wrong.

jonathan-skrmetti.notes_set: TN AG (28th, appointed by TN Supreme Court Aug 2022, term to Sept 2030). Defended TN minors' gender-procedure ban at SCOTUS (US v. Skrmetti, won 6-3, 2025). HARD record: anti-trans-ideology, porn/social-media age-verification, ESG (BlackRock suit), FDA/ATF/Title IX/HIPAA/birthright federal-overreach litigation. DEVIATIONS: defending/appealing TN gun-carry-restriction statutes in Hughes v. Lee (criticized by gun-rights groups); 2022 opinion permits discarding unimplanted IVF embryos. De-finalized 2026-06.

0.note: No conception-personhood statement; opinion held ban doesn't protect pre-implantation embryos.

1.note: Defends existing TN ban; no abolitionist stance documented.

2.note: Official opinion permits discarding unimplanted IVF embryos under TN law.

4.note: Joined 21-state amicus defending biological-female sports teams.

profile.confidence_note: Scored from RI Lt. Gov record + RI.gov press releases + endorsements (2021-2026). Confirmed incumbent seeking reelection; Democratic primary 2026-09-08.

sabina-matos.notes_set: RI Lt. Governor (70th, since Apr 2021), first Dominican American elected statewide in U.S. Confirmed running for reelection (Feb 2026 kickoff); primary 2026-09-08. Documented progressive record: co-championed Equality in Abortion Coverage Act (Medicaid abortion funding), Let RI Vote Act (no-excuse mail/drop boxes), assault-weapons sales ban; EMILY's List-backed since 2010; pro-immigrant/sanctuary advocacy. No conservative-side deviations found. De-finalized 2026-06.

tony-venhuizen.notes_set: SD Lt. Governor (40th, appointed Jan 2025 after Noem→DHS/Rhoden→Gov), former SD House Dist. 13 (2023-2025), ex-chief-of-staff to Govs. Daugaard & Noem. On 2026 Rhoden ticket + campaign chair. Sparse moral-issue fingerprint (budget/education/operations legislator). HARD: prime-sponsored HB1055 (raised Opportunity Scholarship). DEVIATION: Dec 2024 proposal to raise state sales tax to 5% (a Republican proposing a tax increase). Most cells null — roll-call votes on SD culture-war bills (HB1080 gender care, HB1231 foreign farmland) not in accessible cited sources. De-finalized 2026-06.

profile.confidence_note: Scored from SD AG official actions/litigation + news (2009-2026). CANDIDACY CORRECTION: not seeking AG reelection — won 2026-06-02 GOP primary for SD's at-large U.S. House seat; faces Dem Nicole Gronli 2026-11-03. Possible duplicate federal at-large SD House record — flag for dedup check.

marty-jackley.notes_set: SD AG (33rd; current term since 2023, also AG 2009-2019), Catholic, strongly pro-life career prosecutor. Won GOP primary for SD's lone U.S. House seat (June 2 2026); faces Dem Gronli Nov 3. HARD record: anti-abortion enforcement (Mayday Health suit, HB1274 ad ban, mifepristone brief), defended near-total ban; anti-trans-ideology litigation (ACA 1557, Title IX, locker rooms); pro-death-penalty/law-and-order; ATF/FDA/Title IX federal-overreach suits; 287(g) ICE + birthright-citizenship amicus. DEVIATION: 2015 opinion that county clerks have no 1A right to refuse same-sex licenses. De-finalized 2026-06.
